articles.title: The Baby Race That Shocked Canada
articles.summary: Imagine a contest where the winner is the mom with the most babies! That's what happened in Canada during the Great Depression. A rich man named Charles Millar left money to the woman who had the most children in 10 years. The contest was called the Great Stork Derby. It caused huge fights and newspaper stories. Some people thought it was funny, but others were angry because they thought big families were bad. The winner got a lot of money, but the contest also showed how people felt about immigrants and poor families.

articles.title: Tennis Star Carlos Alcaraz Out of French Open Due to Wrist Injury
articles.summary: Bad news for tennis fans! Carlos Alcaraz, the 22-year-old champion who won the French Open two years in a row, has to skip the tournament this year. He hurt his right wrist during a match in Barcelona. Doctors say he needs to rest. Alcaraz is sad but says he will come back stronger. His big rival, Jannik Sinner, now has a chance to win the French Open for the first time. Alcaraz has won seven Grand Slam titles already, including the Australian Open this year. He is one of the best tennis players in the world.

articles.title: Green Stamps: The Old-School Way to Get Free Stuff
articles.summary: Before apps and points, there were Green Stamps! When you shopped at certain stores, you got little green stamps. You stuck them in a booklet. When the booklet was full, you could trade it for cool stuff like lamps or bikes. The company S&H started this in 1896. By the 1960s, millions of people used them. But then stores started their own rewards programs, and Green Stamps faded away. Today, loyalty programs are huge — worth billions of dollars. But people still forget to use their points!
